name: Arab League

colors.symbolism: Islam; the traditional colour of pan-Arab and Muslim symbolism
the crescent and the Arabic inscription
the chain of unity surrounding the emblem

description: A green field charged with the central emblem of the Arab League: a white crescent (opening upward) enclosing a white Arabic inscription reading 'League of Arab States' (جامعة الدول العربية), the whole encircled by a gold chain of links symbolising the member states.
history: The Arab League was founded on 22 March 1945 by Egypt, Iraq, Transjordan, Lebanon, Saudi Arabia, Syria, and Yemen. The flag was adopted on 8 March 1945 with a green field and the seal of the League. The chain originally bore a different number of links matching the founding membership; the 22-link version reflects the membership level at the flag's later standardization. Earlier variants used red or black chains and gold or black script; the modern design with white crescent, white inscription and gold chain on green is the version in current use.

features.symbolism: Islam and the colour traditionally associated with Arab and Muslim civilization

features.role: Seal of the Arab League (gold chain of links)
features.symbolism: the union of Arab states, with the chain's twenty-two links representing the member states at the time of the flag's adoption
Islam; cultural and religious heritage of the Arab world
Reads جامعة الدول العربية ('League of Arab States') within the crescent
